A single white bloom, / on the barren, ashen plain, / beauty born of loss.
Empty coffers chime, / a hollow anthem of want, / despair’s cold refrain.
The butcher’s sharp steel, / gleaming bright with borrowed life, / a feast for the void.
Lichen stains the wall, / a muted green apology, / for empires decayed.
Scars map every soul, / whispers of battles long past, / we carry the weight.
Broken wings still dream, / of soaring on winds of grace, / bound by earthly chains.
The beggar’s soft gaze, / pierces through the armored heart, / a plea lost in stone.
A lone wolf’s lament, / echoes through the haunted wood, / hunger’s bitter cry.
Gold dust on the lips, / a final, empty comfort, / as the darkness falls.
The warrior’s last breath, / a prayer lost upon the breeze, / for a world undone.
Stone walls cannot shield, / from the rot within the heart, / decay's slow embrace.
Faded silk remains, / a ghost of opulent dreams, / beauty turned to dust.

Pale moon on the snow, / Each flake a fallen soldier, / Silence fills the land.
A hunger remains, / Not for bread or wine or warmth, / But for what is lost.

The river flows on, / Carrying grief with the silt, / Towards a grey sea.

Scarred wood of the gate, / Guards a path no one travels, / Silence settles deep.
A single firefly, / Briefly lights the darkened wood, / A soul’s flickering hope.
The wind’s mournful song, / Through the branches, bare and cold, / Ancestors are near.
Dust on a piano, / Untouched melodies arise, / Ghosts dance in the hall.

The river remembers, / All the songs the stones have heard, / Secrets held within.
Wind whispers through reeds, / A forgotten battle cry, / Echoes in the mist.
Grain falls to the earth, / A promise of golden days, / Hope in every seed.
A lone candle burns, / Against the encroaching dark— / Fate's fragile flicker.

A rusted sword hilt, / A silent testament to, / Battles fought and lost.
Empty coffers ring, / A hollow sound of despair, / Wealth’s fleeting embrace.
Grey stone, moss-grown walls, / An old man’s gaze fixed afar, / Waiting for the dark.
Ripple of a sigh, / Across a face etched with time, / Peace finally found.
